cs.AI: Covers all areas of AI except Vision, Robotics, Machine Learning, Multi-agent Systems and NLP; includes expert systems, theorem proving, knowledge representation, planning and reasoning under uncertainty.
cs.CC: Models of computation, complexity classes, structural complexity, trade-offs, upper and lower bounds.
cs.CE: Interdisciplinary computational engineering, finance and science; focuses on large-scale simulation and high-performance platforms.
cs.CR: Cryptography and security, including authentication, public-key systems, proof-carrying code, etc.
cs.CV: Computer vision and pattern recognition, including image processing and scene understanding.
cs.CY: Computers-and-society topics, ethics, policy and education.
cs.DC: Distributed, parallel and cluster computing (fault-tolerance, algorithms, clusters …).
cs.DL: Digital-library design, document and text creation.
cs.DM: Discrete mathematics (combinatorics, graph theory, probabilistic methods).
cs.DS: Data structures and algorithms (design and analysis).
cs.ET: Emerging (post-CMOS) information-processing technologies and associated modeling / design questions.
cs.GL: General CS literature – surveys, predictions, biographies, miscellaneous.
cs.GT: Computer-science & game-theory intersection: mechanism design, learning in games, etc.
cs.HC: Human–computer interaction, user interfaces, collaborative computing.
cs.LG: Machine learning (supervised, unsupervised, RL, robustness, fairness, applications).
cs.LO: Logic in CS: finite-model theory, program logics, verification, etc.
cs.MA: Multi-agent systems and distributed artificial intelligence.
cs.NE: Neural and evolutionary computing (neural nets, genetic algorithms, ALife).
cs.OH: Other CS – use when nothing else fits.
cs.PF: Performance measurement, evaluation, queueing and simulation.
cs.SD: Sound computing and audio information processing.
cs.SE: Software engineering: design tools, metrics, testing, environments.
cs.SI: Social and information networks: design, analysis, modeling and applications.

econ.EM: Econometrics: theoretical, micro-, macro- and methodological aspects of statistical inference in economics.
econ.GN: General economics: methodological, applied and empirical contributions.
econ.TH: Theoretical economics: contract, decision and game theory, general equilibrium, growth, macro etc.

eess.AS: Audio and speech processing (analysis, synthesis, enhancement, recognition …).
eess.IV: Image and video processing (capture, communication, analysis and display).
eess.SP: Signal processing: theory, algorithms and applications to all signal modalities.
eess.SY: Systems and control (modeling, simulation, optimization; nonlinear, adaptive, hybrid, robust control, CPS, etc.)

physics.physics.data-an: Data analysis, statistics & probability in physics.

stat.ML: Machine learning with statistical / theoretical grounding.
